fact: February 13 is exactly 44 days away from the spring equinox - that's like counting down 44 sleeps until spring officially arrives!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is when maple trees start getting ready to make sap - the sweet stuff that becomes maple syrup on your pancakes!
February 13 falls smack in the middle of 'mud season' in cold places - when snow melts during the day but freezes again at night, creating the slippiest, gooiest mess ever.
Groundhogs are getting seriously hungry right now because they've been sleeping for months and their fat stores are running low - no wonder they're grumpy when they wake up!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, this date marks peak summer heat - swimming pools are at their warmest and ice cream trucks work overtime.
When there's a full moon around February 13, it's called a 'Snow Moon' because that's when the deepest snow typically piles up in North America.
Skunk cabbage plants are so weird they can melt snow around them by generating their own heat - and they start doing this right around mid-February!
February 13 is day 44 of the year, which means there are exactly 321 days left until New Year's Eve - that's like having 10 and a half months of adventures ahead!
In places like Alaska, the sun is finally staying up long enough that kids can play outside after school without needing flashlights - winter is losing its grip!
People born on February 13 are Aquarians - the zodiac sign known for being creative water-bearers whose season ends soon as Pisces approaches.
This is prime time for 'thunder snow' in cold places - when it snows so hard that lightning and thunder happen at the same time, creating the most epic winter light show ever.
